target.name: 9 Mothers
target.founder: Russell Smith, Roman Khomenko, Bogdan Pyzh

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $4M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
9 Mothers is a seed-stage defense and security company founded by Russell Smith, Roman Khomenko, and Bogdan Pyzh that has raised $4.5M to date. The broader sector comprises 2 public comps (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers including Anduril Industries, Helsing, and Shield AI, though the seed-stage cohort within this universe is too narrow for robust peer-median analysis.

Given data limitations, stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable approach. Using total raised ($4.5M) as a valuation proxy and applying a standard 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement yields $11.25M. This reflects the venture capital convention of marking companies up meaningfully upon achieving early milestones and reduced technical risk.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $10M to $13M, with a median of $11M. This range is derived entirely from stage-progression methodology; peer-median triangulation proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow defense tech category. Key uncertainties center on the timing and probability of the company's next fundraising event, product-market validation metrics, and customer traction—none of which are quantified in available data. Any revision would require additional intelligence on near-term funding milestones or revenue performance.
